subject: Sociology
title: Why Sociology Matters Today
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Why Sociology Matters Today.

hook.text: Inequality is rising. Trust in institutions is falling. The questions sociology asks have never been more urgent.

buildup.text: Sociology helps explain polarization, racial injustice, health disparities, and why some communities thrive.

discovery.text: Policymakers use sociological research to design interventions for poverty, education, and public health.

twist.text: Yet most people have never taken a sociology course. The tools exist, but public understanding lags far behind.

climax.text: Sociology doesn't just describe problems. It reveals the hidden mechanisms that produce them, enabling real change.

punchline.text: Understanding society is the first step to improving it.

quiz.question: How does sociology contribute to solving social problems?
options: By revealing hidden mechanisms that produce them
By offering personal therapy to individuals
